ttl.name: VEHICLE STABILITY CONTROL SYSTEM (for ADVICS Made)

para.dtcname: Open or Short in Acceleration Sensor Circuit
Acceleration Sensor Output Voltage Malfunction (Test Mode DTC)
Yaw Rate and / or Acceleration Sensor Power Supply Voltage Malfunction

content5.ptxt: The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system.
The yaw rate sensor has a built in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle's condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2).

If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, the DTC U0123/62 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124/95 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are output.
These DTCs are also output when calibration has not been completed.

DTCs C0371/71 and C1279/79 can be deleted when the yaw rate and acceleration sensor sends a yaw rate and/or acceleration signal or the Test Mode ends. DTCs C0371/71 and C1279/79 are output only in the Test Mode.

entry.ptxt: At a vehicle speed of 6 mph (10 km/h) or more, either GL1 or GL2 (input signal) does not change for 30 seconds or more.

item.ptxt: Yaw rate and acceleration sensor circuit

entry.ptxt: Sensor malfunction signal is received from the yaw rate sensor.

item.ptxt: Yaw rate and acceleration sensor circuit

entry.ptxt: While the vehicle speed changes from 19 mph (30 km/h) to 0 mph (0 km/h), the condition that the values of GL1 and GL2 do not change occurs 16 times or more.

item.ptxt: Yaw rate and acceleration sensor circuit

entry.ptxt: When either of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: A data malfunction signal is received from the acceleration sensor.
After the difference between GL1 and GL2 becomes 0.6 G or more with the vehicle stationary, the difference remains 0.4 G or more for 60 seconds or more.
Yaw rate and acceleration sensor circuit

entry.ptxt: At a vehicle speed of 19 mph (30 km/h) or more, the difference between the forward and backward G calculated from the acceleration sensor value and that calculated from the vehicle speed sensor exceeds 0.35 G for 60 seconds or more.

item.ptxt: Yaw rate and acceleration sensor circuit

entry.ptxt: At a vehicle speed of more than 2 mph (3 km/h), the acceleration sensor power source malfunction signal is received for 10 seconds or more.

item.ptxt: Yaw rate and acceleration sensor power source circuit

atten3.ptxt: When replacing the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, perform zero point calibration (See page ).

atten4.ptxt: When U0123/62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 is output together with C1232/32, C1234/34, C1243/43, C1244/44, C1245/45, or C1381/97, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0123/62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 first.

test1.ptxt: At a speed of 19 mph (30 km/h) or more, drive the vehicle, turn the steering wheel, and decelerate the vehicle (depress the brake pedal).
Turn the ignition switch on (IG) again and check that no CAN communication system DTC is output.
Check if DTCs for zero point calibration of the yaw rate sensor undone (C1210/36) or for zero point calibration of the acceleration sensor undone (C1336/39) are output.

entry.ptxt: DTCs (C1210/36, C1336/39 and/or CAN communication system DTC) are not output
CAN communication system DTC is output

testgrp.testtitle: CHECK YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR INSTALLATION

test1.ptxt: Check that the yaw rate and acceleration sensor has been installed properly (See page ).

specitem.ptxt: The sensor is tightened to the specified torque.

testgrp.testtitle: INSPECT YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR (IG TERMINAL)

test1.ptxt: Disconnect the yaw rate and acceleration sensor connector.
Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below.

testgrp.testtitle: INSPECT YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR (GND TERMINAL)

test1.ptxt: Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below.

atten3.ptxt: Check the yaw rate and acceleration sensor signal after replacement (See page ).

atten4.ptxt: If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the Problem Symptoms Table, refer back to the table and proceed to the next step (See page ).

testgrp.testtitle: REPLACE YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR
REPAIR CIRCUIT INDICATED BY OUTPUT DTC
INSTALL YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR CORRECTLY
REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (IG CIRCUIT)
REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (GND CIRCUIT)
